message:
feat(ci,search): add workflow_dispatch to release, harden tag validation, and suppress unused_mut warnings

Release workflow improvements:
- Add workflow_dispatch input so releases can be triggered manually
  with an explicit tag parameter (useful when tag-push automation
  misses or needs a re-run)
- Extract version from either refs/tags/ or the dispatch input,
  failing fast when neither is present
- Add a "Validate tag matches Cargo.toml version" step that compares
  the tag against cargo metadata, preventing accidental mismatched
  releases
- Fix the crates.io publish gate: use the release job's output
  version instead of github.ref so pre-release detection works for
  both push and dispatch triggers
- Emit a warning annotation when CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN is missing
  rather than silently skipping the publish step

Dependency change:
- Enable the "crossterm" feature on the ftui path dependency in
  Cargo.toml to align with the frankenTUI crossterm backend migration

Search module lint fixes:
- Add #[allow(unused_mut)] to three mutex lock sites in
  fastembed_embedder.rs and fastembed_reranker.rs where the binding
  must be mut for the lock guard but the compiler warns it is never
  mutated — suppresses false-positive warnings without changing
  behavior

Documentation:
- Append the standard "About Contributions" notice to README.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,6 +7,12 @@ on:
   push:
     tags:
       - "v*"
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      tag:
+        description: "Tag to release (e.g., v0.2.0)"
+        required: true
+        type: string
 
 env:
   CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
@@ -129,7 +135,28 @@ jobs:
 
       - name: Extract version from tag
         id: version
-        run: echo "version=${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          if [[ "${GITHUB_REF}" == refs/tags/* ]]; then
+            RAW_TAG="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"
+          else
+            RAW_TAG="${{ inputs.tag }}"
+          fi
+          if [[ -z "${RAW_TAG}" ]]; then
+            echo "Tag is required."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          VERSION="${RAW_TAG#v}"
+          echo "version=${VERSION}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+      - name: Validate tag matches Cargo.toml version
+        shell: bash
+        run: |
+          CARGO_VERSION="$(cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version 1 | jq -r '.packages[0].version')"
+          if [[ "${CARGO_VERSION}" != "${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}" ]]; then
+            echo "Tag version (${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}) does not match Cargo.toml (${CARGO_VERSION})."
+            exit 1
+          fi
 
       - name: List artifacts
         run: |
@@ -222,7 +249,7 @@ jobs:
     needs: release
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     timeout-minutes: 15
-    if: ${{ !contains(github.ref, '-') }}
+    if: ${{ !contains(needs.release.outputs.version, '-') }}
     env:
       CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
     steps:
@@ -235,6 +262,10 @@ jobs:
         if: ${{ env.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN != '' }}
         run: cargo publish --all-features --locked
 
+      - name: Warn when token is missing
+        if: ${{ env.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN == '' }}
+        run: echo "::warning::CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN is not configured; skipping publish."
+
   # ==========================================================================
   # Notify Package Managers to Update
   # ==========================================================================

@@ -2665,4 +2665,8 @@ cargo llvm-cov --all-features --workspace --text
 cargo test --test e2e_filters -- --test-threads=1
 ```
 
+## About Contributions
+
+> *About Contributions:* Please don't take this the wrong way, but I do not accept outside contributions for any of my projects. I simply don't have the mental bandwidth to review anything, and it's my name on the thing, so I'm responsible for any problems it causes; thus, the risk-reward is highly asymmetric from my perspective. I'd also have to worry about other "stakeholders," which seems unwise for tools I mostly make for myself for free. Feel free to submit issues, and even PRs if you want to illustrate a proposed fix, but know I won't merge them directly. Instead, I'll have Claude or Codex review submissions via `gh` and independently decide whether and how to address them. Bug reports in particular are welcome. Sorry if this offends, but I want to avoid wasted time and hurt feelings. I understand this isn't in sync with the prevailing open-source ethos that seeks community contributions, but it's the only way I can move at this velocity and keep my sanity.
+
 ---
